etc/seed.purpose: Demo data seeding layer: Benthos/Redpanda Connect pipeline YAML configs that generate synthetic CloudEvents and POST them to OpenMeter's ingest API (/api/v1/events). Holds the top-level seed.yaml (token usage events) and observability.yaml; the streams/ child holds domain-specific generators (api-requests, prompt-tokens, workload-runtime) that share one skeleton.

patterns.name: CloudEvents 1.0 envelope
patterns.description: Every generated event sets specversion, id (uuid_v4()), type, source, subject, and time at the root; dimensional attributes live inside data: {}, never at envelope root.

patterns.name: Env-var parameterisation with defaults
patterns.description: Every runtime value uses ${VAR:default} so files run out-of-the-box yet stay overridable in CI.

patterns.name: Infinite generation with count: 0
patterns.description: Seed streams use count: ${SEEDER_COUNT:0}; 0 means run indefinitely. A positive count terminates the stream and defeats the seeder.

patterns.name: Subject pool via modulo randomisation seeded from time
patterns.description: Subjects are drawn from a bounded pool (customer-0..N) via random_int(seed: timestamp_unix_nano()) % $max_subjects; always seed from timestamp_unix_nano() — a fixed seed repeats.

patterns.name: Dual-output switch: HTTP primary + optional stdout
patterns.description: output.switch always has an unconditional HTTP POST case (continue: true) and a conditional stdout logger gated by SEEDER_LOG=true; both cases appear in every stream.
patterns.example:
output:
  switch:
    cases:
      - check: ""
        continue: true
        output: { http_client: ... }
      - check: '"${SEEDER_LOG:false}" == "true"'
        output: { stdout: { codec: lines } }

key_file_guides.role: Top-level stream generating 'tokens' CloudEvents (provider/model/type/count dimensions); entry point for `benthos -c etc/seed/seed.yaml`.
key_file_guides.watch_for: count: ${SEEDER_COUNT:0} must stay 0-defaulted. The commented ts_sub_iso8601 backdating block is for historical seeding — leave commented unless intentional.

key_file_guides.role: OTel tracing config for the Benthos seed process; sends traces to the local collector on :4317 with sampling disabled by default.
key_file_guides.watch_for: sampling.enabled defaults to false — enabling it in dev floods the local collector with no user value.

key_file_guides.role: Child: per-domain generators (api-requests, prompt-tokens, workload-runtime) reusing the seed.yaml skeleton.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Each stream must keep the identical input.generate + output.switch structure; structural deviation breaks batch seeding, and dimensions must stay inside data: {}.

anti_patterns: Hardcoding OPENMETER_TOKEN or OPENMETER_BASE_URL — always use ${VAR:default}
Setting count > 0 — seed streams must run indefinitely (count: 0 means infinite)
Putting dimensional attributes outside data: {} — they belong in data, not at the CloudEvent envelope root
Using a fixed random seed instead of timestamp_unix_nano() — produces a repeating non-random sequence
Emitting a non-CloudEvents-1.0 envelope (omitting specversion, id, or time) — breaks ingest API validation

decisions.decision: All streams share one input.generate + output.switch skeleton; only the bloblang mapping body varies
decisions.rationale: Consistency makes adding new event types trivial and prevents structural drift that would break the Benthos pipeline runner.

decisions.decision: Subject space bounded by SEEDER_SUBJECT_COUNT rather than purely random UUIDs
decisions.rationale: Bounded subjects produce realistic per-customer usage curves and let meter aggregations return non-sparse results in the demo UI.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new seed stream for a new event type
